# redis_hash (Resource)

Manages a Redis hash (`HSET`).

Changing `key` destroys and recreates the resource. On update, fields removed from the configuration are deleted with `HDEL`; new and modified fields are written with `HSET`.

On `Create`, the provider issues a `DEL` on the target key before writing. This guarantees Terraform fully owns the key's contents. To adopt a key that already exists in Redis without losing its current data, use `terraform import` instead.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "redis_hash" "example" {
  key = "user:42"
  fields = {
    name  = "Alice"
    email = "alice@example.com"
    role  = "admin"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->

## Schema

### Required

- `key` (String) The Redis key.
- `fields` (Map of String) A map of field names to string values for the hash. Must contain at least one entry.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The Redis key, used as the resource identifier.

## Import

Any existing Redis hash key can be imported using the key name. All current fields are imported into state.

```shell
terraform import redis_hash.example "user:42"
```
